title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: Drifloon package helps download Canadian hourly weather station data Environment Climate Change Canada portal. Core functions: load_metadata(): Load station inventory used download wrappers. sync_metadata(): Checks updates metadata official station inventory CSV. download_station_month(): Download one station, one year, one month. download_station_by_name(): Download one station year range. download_station_province(): Download stations one province. download_all_station(): Download stations metadata. download_metadata(): Copy packaged metadata .rds output folder.

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: metadata frame expected include key columns: Name: name station. Note: stations name different station ids. Province: Province station. Station.ID: unique identifier station HLY.First.Year: first year available hourly data HLY.Last.Year: last year available hourly data
code: library(Drifloon)  meta <- load_metadata() head(meta[, c("Name", "Province", "Station.ID", "HLY.First.Year", "HLY.Last.Year")]) ##                    Name         Province Station.ID HLY.First.Year ## 1     ESQUIMALT HARBOUR BRITISH COLUMBIA         52           1994 ## 2               MALAHAT BRITISH COLUMBIA         65           1994 ## 3     SATURNA ISLAND CS BRITISH COLUMBIA         96           1994 ## 4 VICTORIA GONZALES HTS BRITISH COLUMBIA        113           1953 ## 5  VICTORIA GONZALES CS BRITISH COLUMBIA        114           1994 ## 6      VICTORIA INT'L A BRITISH COLUMBIA        118           1953 ##   HLY.Last.Year ## 1          2026 ## 2          2025 ## 3          2026 ## 4          2000 ## 5          2026 ## 6          2013

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: Use sync_metadata() pull recent station metadata update local metadata. change new station metadata available. want happen load, use:
code: # Typical sync with defaults sync_result <- sync_metadata() str(sync_result)  # Example with tighter filtering and backup control sync_result <- sync_metadata(   provinces = c("ON", "QC"),   min_year = 2000,   create_backup = TRUE,   max_backup_bytes = 25 * 1024^2 ) meta <- load_metadata(sync = TRUE)

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: want local copy packaged metadata, use download_metadata().

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: Start small verify names, IDs, output paths. default download directory root current working directory folder drifloon_output/.
code: # By station name ok <- download_station_month(   station_name = "TORONTO CITY",   year = 2020,   month = 1,   out_dir = "drifloon_output" )  # By station ID (useful when names are duplicated) ok <- download_station_month(   station_id = 31688,   year = 2020,   month = 1,   out_dir = "drifloon_output" )

what: Download a station across a year range
title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: asking station data every month year range 2018 2020. Note can use parallel=TRUE argument want parallelize download, make faster. multiple stations name. case pass station_name station_id force specific match.
code: download_station_by_name(   station_name = "TORONTO CITY",   first_year = 2018,   last_year = 2020,   out_dir = "drifloon_output",   parallel = FALSE )

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: large download download_station_province() typically , estimates total files storage first.
code: # Interactive confirmation (default) download_station_province(   province = "ON",   first_year = 2019,   last_year = 2020,   out_dir = "drifloon_output" )  # Non-interactive workflows (scripts/CI) download_station_province(   province = "ON",   first_year = 2019,   last_year = 2020,   out_dir = "drifloon_output",   confirm = TRUE )

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: Use cautiously: can generate large downloads.
code: download_all_station(   first_year = 2019,   last_year = 2020,   out_dir = "drifloon_output",   confirm = TRUE )

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: default directory current working directory can specified ot anything like.

title: Introduction to Drifloon
text: .load_station_metadata() .resolve_out_dir() .sync_station_metadata() .normalize_station_name_key() .find_station_name_index() .province_normalize() .validate_year() .estimate_download_size() .check_disk_space() .is_valid_downloaded_month_file() can inspect internals debugging triple-colon access:

title: Introduction to Drifloon
code: library(Drifloon)  # 1) Refresh metadata sync_metadata(provinces = c("ON", "QC"), min_year = 2000)  # 2) Load filtered metadata meta <- load_metadata()  # 3) Validate one month first download_station_month(   station_name = "TORONTO CITY",   year = 2020,   month = 1,   out_dir = "drifloon_output" )  # 4) Scale to a station range download_station_by_name(   station_name = "TORONTO CITY",   first_year = 2018,   last_year = 2020,   out_dir = "drifloon_output" )  # 5) Scale to province only after checks download_station_province(   province = "ON",   first_year = 2019,   last_year = 2020,   out_dir = "drifloon_output",   confirm = TRUE )  # 6) Create relational database create_database()

title: Weather Downloader
text: Drifloon helps discover download historical hourly weather data Environment Climate Change Canada (ECCC), optionally build local SQLite database analysis. https://climate.weather.gc.ca/historical_data/search_historic_data_e.html

title: Weather Downloader
text: Load station metadata (name, station ID, province, hourly year range). Sync station metadata official station inventory. Download one month, one station, one province, stations. Build SQLite database downloaded CSV files.

text: downloads smart look list stations, download requires either station name, station_id, province.

what: Create a SQLite weather database from local files — create_database
title: Create a SQLite weather database from local files — create_database
text: Builds SQLite database using station metadata hourly station CSV files.

title: Create a SQLite weather database from local files — create_database
code: create_database(   base_dir = file.path(getwd()),   db_name = "climate_database.db",   overwrite = FALSE,   batch_size = 50L )

title: Create a SQLite weather database from local files — create_database
text: base_dir Character. Root directory contains input files metadata hourly data files located. db_name Character. SQLite file name create file.path(base_dir, "database"). Default "climate_database.db". overwrite Logical. TRUE, replaces existing database file. batch_size Numeric. Number source files accumulate observation-table write. Default 50.

title: Create a SQLite weather database from local files — create_database
text: Invisibly returns list created path row/file counts.

title: Create a SQLite weather database from local files — create_database
text: Expected file structure base_dir: data/HLY_station_info.rds drifloon_output/ (recursive station CSV files) Station metadata read file.path(base_dir, "data", "HLY_station_info.rds"). Column names matched permissively support common variants (example, Station.ID Station_ID). Hourly rows matched Climate ID using climate-id column CSV (example, Climate.ID Climate ID). SQLite database file created file.path(base_dir, "database"), directly base_dir. current working directory contains 1000 CSV files, interactive confirmation prompt shown resulting database may large. Station records pre-filtered Climate IDs found downloaded hourly CSV files insertion. station metadata rows present, function still creates database schema weather lookup table.

what: Estimate download size for a batch of downloads — .estimate_download_size
title: Estimate download size for a batch of downloads — .estimate_download_size
text: Calculates estimated number files space (MB) downloaded given set stations year range.

title: Estimate download size for a batch of downloads — .estimate_download_size
code: .estimate_download_size(   stations,   first_year = NULL,   last_year = NULL,   bytes_per_file = 130000 )

title: Estimate download size for a batch of downloads — .estimate_download_size
text: stations Data frame containing station metadata columns HLY.First.Year HLY.Last.Year. first_year Numeric. Starting year. NULL, uses station metadata. last_year Numeric. Ending year. NULL, uses station metadata. bytes_per_file Numeric. Expected size .csv file bytes. Default 130000 (roughly 130 KB).

title: Estimate download size for a batch of downloads — .estimate_download_size
text: List elements: count (number files), size_mb (estimated MB),   size_gb (estimated GB), years (number years).

what: Sync station metadata from official inventory CSV — .sync_station_metadata
title: Sync station metadata from official inventory CSV — .sync_station_metadata
text: Downloads current station inventory CSV, applies filtering, compares current local metadata, updates local CSV/RDS files content changed (forced).

title: Sync station metadata from official inventory CSV — .sync_station_metadata
code: .sync_station_metadata(   station_csv_url =     "https://collaboration.cmc.ec.gc.ca/cmc/climate/Get_More_Data_Plus_de_donnees/Station%20Inventory%20EN.csv",   csv_path = file.path("extdata", "HLY_station_info.csv"),   rds_path = file.path("data", "HLY_station_info.rds"),   force = FALSE,   create_backup = TRUE,   max_backup_bytes = 25 * 1024^2,   require_hourly = TRUE,   provinces = NULL,   min_year = NULL,   max_year = NULL,   downloader = download.file )

title: Sync station metadata from official inventory CSV — .sync_station_metadata
text: station_csv_url Character. Source URL station inventory CSV. csv_path Character. Local output path filtered CSV metadata. rds_path Character. Local output path filtered RDS metadata. force Logical. TRUE, writes files even data unchanged. create_backup Logical. TRUE, backup current metadata update current metadata valid small enough. max_backup_bytes Numeric. Maximum combined size current CSV+RDS files eligible backup. Default 25 MB. require_hourly Logical. TRUE, keep rows hourly ranges. provinces Character vector provinces keep (optional). min_year Numeric. Minimum year overlap filter. max_year Numeric. Optional maximum year overlap filter. downloader Function used download files (default: download.file).

title: Sync station metadata from official inventory CSV — .sync_station_metadata
text: Invisibly returns list update status, modified-date text,   output paths.

text: Checks year values valid (numeric, negative, far future).

Validate year inputs — .validate_year
text: year Numeric NULL. Year value(s) validate. allow_null Logical. TRUE, NULL accepted. Default TRUE.

title: Validate year inputs — .validate_year
text: validated year integer, NULL allow_null=TRUE input NULL.

what: Download hourly data for all stations — download_all_station
title: Download hourly data for all stations — download_all_station
text: Iterates stations metadata data frame downloads hourly data Environment Canada website.

title: Download hourly data for all stations — download_all_station
code: download_all_station(   station_data = NULL,   out_dir = NULL,   first_year = NULL,   last_year = NULL,   parallel = FALSE,   confirm = FALSE )

title: Download hourly data for all stations — download_all_station
text: station_data Data frame station metadata. Optional; omitted, packaged metadata loaded automatically. out_dir Character. Base output directory. supplied, defaults file.path(getwd(), "drifloon_output"). first_year Numeric. Optional starting year. last_year Numeric. Optional ending year. parallel Logical. TRUE, stations downloaded parallel using furrr::future_pwalk. Default FALSE. confirm Logical. FALSE (default), displays estimated download size requests user confirmation. TRUE, skips confirmation prompt proceeds download.

title: Download hourly data for all stations — download_all_station
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Download hourly data for all stations — download_all_station
text: Use caution, trigger large number downloads. default, estimated file count space requirement shown downloading begins. Set confirm = TRUE skip warning.

Copy packaged metadata file to disk — download_metadata

title: Copy packaged metadata file to disk — download_metadata
text: Character path copied .rds file (invisibly).

what: Download full available hourly dataset for a single station — download_station
title: Download full available hourly dataset for a single station — download_station
text: Internal download engine used exported wrappers [download_station_by_name()].

title: Download full available hourly dataset for a single station — download_station
code: download_station(   station,   out_dir = NULL,   first_year = NULL,   last_year = NULL,   parallel = FALSE )

title: Download full available hourly dataset for a single station — download_station
text: station List named vector containing station metadata. Must include Station.ID, Name, Province, HLY.First.Year, HLY.Last.Year. out_dir Character. Base directory station folders created. supplied, defaults file.path(getwd(), "drifloon_output"). first_year Numeric. First year download (default: station metadata). last_year Numeric. Last year download (default: station metadata). parallel Logical. TRUE, downloads months parallel using furrr::future_pwalk. Default FALSE.

title: Download full available hourly dataset for a single station — download_station
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Download full available hourly dataset for a single station — download_station
text: Year ranges validated station metadata. requested years exceed available data, error thrown. Data downloaded year-month pair (months 1 12), sequentially default parallel parallel = TRUE.

TRUE, months downloaded parallel using furrr::future_pwalk. Default FALSE.

what: Drifloon: Canadian Weather Data Download Toolkit — Drifloon

title: Drifloon: Canadian Weather Data Download Toolkit — Drifloon
text: download_station_month(): Download one month hourly data station. download_station_by_name(): Primary user entry station downloads. download_station_province(): Download stations province. download_all_station(): Download stations metadata. load_metadata(): Load packaged station metadata. sync_metadata(): Sync metadata official station inventory. download_metadata(): Copy packaged metadata .rds file disk.

title: Drifloon: Canadian Weather Data Download Toolkit — Drifloon
